ps140: 4, Podoben “As one valiant among the martyrs…”

After Your Resurrection from the tomb,* You stood among Your friends* despite the doors being locked,* O All-powerful One!* And as Thomas did not believe,* You showed him Your wounds and Your side, pierced by a spear,* strengthening him and giving him faith, O Word,* that it was indeed You,* Who suffered the salvific Passion** because of Your great mercy.

Having seen my powerlessness* because of my great condescension and immeasurable mercy* do not doubt but have faith, O Twin,* Says the Lord,* As it is Me Who suffered and rose from the tomb on the third day,* and Who emptied the treasure of Hades** and gave life to those who died since the beginning.

Marvelling at seeing You,* Thomas cried out to You, O All-powerful One:* You my God and my Lord,* I do believe, O Lover of humankind,* that it was You who suffered* and who heals passions of our souls!* I bow before Your power* and proclaim to the world** Your awe-inspiring and all-powerful Resurrection.

The disciples were assembled on the eighth day,* and the Saviour appeared to them!* He gave them peace and said to Thomas:* Come, apostle! Feel my hands, which were pierced by the nails!* Most wonderful doubt of Thomas!* It brought the hearts of the faithful to knowledge!* My Lord and my God, glory to You!
aposticha: After Your Resurrection, O Lord,* while Your disciples were gathered and the doors were closed,* You stood among them, granting them peace.* And Thomas believed through seeing Your hands and Your side* and confessed You as Lord and God* Who saves those who hope in You,** O Lover of mankind.
